behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure commonly occurs at module handoff (HTTP raw-body capture -> signature verifier -> enqueue gate), not only inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is required; in-process HTTP + real middleware wiring is enough. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests that pass precomputed raw body can miss bugs where middleware alters body bytes. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network/subprocess or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler in-process with the real request-parsing/middleware stack; send a request where X-Signature-256 is computed for different bytes than the sent raw body; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue writer was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module ordering (handler <-> persistent queue). REAL-INFRA: local persistence (DB/file) can be exercised in-process; no external OS/network/subprocess boundary is required. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing queue write as immediate success can hide ordering bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real remote service or OS primitive beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use real queue implementation against local test storage; make queue write intentionally block until a latch is released; assert HTTP 200 is not observed before write commit signal, then is observed after commit.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in orchestration between enqueue/dispatch and downstream worker lifecycle. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive is necessary; controlled in-process slow worker is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests over a single function can miss accidental await/Promise chaining across modules. Rejected System: behavior text is about await semantics, not real subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler, queue, and a downstream processor that sleeps; send valid event; assert response is returned before processor completion marker while event is still eventually processed.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is a wall-clock SLO under concurrency; scheduler/event-loop contention and real HTTP serving behavior are part of the failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/in-process stubs can pass while real server under concurrent load breaches latency. Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock timing under concurrent slow work, which integration fakes may not reproduce reliably.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real HTTP service process, pre-load it with slow concurrent downstream work, then send new webhook requests over real HTTP and measure end-to-end latency; assert responses stay under 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness is at state handoff boundary (handler -> queue/idempotency store), not just pure logic. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process modules plus local persistent store. MOCK-HIDE: simplistic mocks may miss duplicate-write paths tied to persistence semantics. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real remote network/subprocess; local persistence suffices.
behaviors.test_description: Use real dedupe/persistent queue modules with local test storage; submit same event ID twice; assert only one queue record exists and second request is handled per contract without second write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal across module boundary into HTTP contract. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process queue module can emit full condition. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only response builder may miss actual error propagation path from queue layer. Rejected System: behavior text says 'queue reports it is full' (already-signaled condition), not real capacity mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue module wired; force queue to return its canonical 'full' error/state; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is queue + persistence state behavior across modules, not just handler logic. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough; no external network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue cannot validate real capacity accounting bugs. Rejected System: behavior text is local queue capacity enforcement, which fits integration with local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity in test storage; enqueue up to limit then one extra; assert extra enqueue is rejected and persisted depth does not exceed configured cap.
